findings.title: North Star Metric: User Engagement
findings.description: The North Star metric for SmartNotify is the Daily Active Users (DAU), which reflects the number of unique users engaging with the product daily. This metric is crucial as it indicates overall product adoption and user retention.
assumptions: DAU is a reliable indicator of user engagement and product value.
recommendations: Monitor DAU trends weekly to assess user engagement.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR)
findings.description: The Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR) measures the percentage of notifications that users engage with. This is a key performance indicator for assessing the effectiveness of notification content and delivery.
assumptions: Higher CTR indicates better notification relevance and timing.
recommendations: Aim to increase CTR by optimizing notification content.

metadata.measurement_method: Analytics tracking of user interactions with notifications

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Unsubscribe Rate
findings.description: The Unsubscribe Rate measures the percentage of users opting out of notifications. This metric serves as a guardrail to ensure that user fatigue does not increase, indicating potential issues with notification frequency or relevance.
assumptions: A lower unsubscribe rate indicates better user satisfaction with notifications.
recommendations: Keep unsubscribe rate below 0.1% to maintain user engagement.

findings.title: Measurement Gap: Notification Delivery Latency
findings.description: Currently, there is a lack of detailed tracking on the notification delivery latency beyond the p95 metric. More granular data is needed to identify performance bottlenecks and improve user experience.
assumptions: Understanding latency at different percentiles will help optimize delivery.

evidence.excerpt: Reduce notification delivery latency from p95 340ms to under 200ms

recommendations: Implement tracking for p50 and p90 latency metrics.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: Notification Timing Optimization
findings.description: Conduct A/B testing on different notification delivery timings to determine optimal engagement windows for users. This will help refine the ML model for notification batching.
assumptions: User engagement varies significantly by time of day.
recommendations: Run experiments with statistical significance of 95% confidence level.

summary: The analysis identifies the Daily Active Users as the North Star metric, with supporting KPIs including Notification CTR and a guardrail metric of Unsubscribe Rate. Gaps in measurement for notification delivery latency and opportunities for experimentation in notification timing were also highlighted.
